reasoning: The threshold for this question is extremely high. The recent reference value is 57.75. A 10x spike would require reaching nearly 578 non-violent protests within a specific 30-day window. This is roughly 19-20 protests on average every single day over a 30 day period. While Sri Lanka experienced massive daily protests during the 2022 political and economic crisis (Aragalaya), generating this sheer volume of independently logged ACLED events is an extreme outlier, likely representing the absolute peak of a once-in-a-generation crisis. The probability of such an event occurring—and specifically randomly aligning with one of the arbitrary fixed 30-day resolution windows—is very low. For near terms (2026), there is no evidence of an impending crisis capable of triggering nearly 600 protests in a month. For more distant dates, the probability increases slightly, reflecting the inherent geopolitical and economic uncertainties and the historical precedent of cyclical instability in Sri Lanka, though it remains under 10%.
